title: Axolus angustifolius
intro:
I couldn't find any information on Axolus angustifolius. However, I think you might be referring to Axolotl is not a plant, it is an amphibian (Mexican salamander), but its scientific name is Ambystoma mexicanum. 

But if you're looking for a plant with the Latin name that has the word 'angustifolius', I found that Achillea millefolium (also known as Yarrow or Milfoil) has the Latin name of 'A. angustifolia' and 'A. millefolium' is another subspecies of the same plant. If you're referring to the other subspecies (A. angustifolia), here's a paragraph about the plant Achillea millefolium:

Achillea millefolium, commonly known as Yarrow or Milfoil, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it beneficial for wound healing, digestive issues, and menstrual cramps. The bioactive constituents of Yarrow include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Yarrow include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, from fever and colds to digestive problems and skin conditions.
uses: I couldn't find any information on a plant species called "Axolus angustifolius".
